commerceProducts.title: Alaskan Crab Brunch with Mimosa Flight
commerceProducts.description: Tantalize your taste buds (and your sense of exploration) with this flavorful Ketchikan shore excursion, as you visit a historical lodge for a delicious crab brunch, featuring local recipes and a flight of mimosas.
highlights: Your Alaskan journey begins with a scenic drive, passing by an old sawmill, majestic totem poles, and cascading waterfalls on your way to the George Inlet Lodge, a charming retreat that once served as a cannery bunkhouse in the early 1900s.

Here, prepare for a mouthwatering brunch, featuring a delicious crab casserole and half an Alaskan crab, accompanied by reindeer sausage, blueberry scones, smoked salmon dip, and a variety flight of three mimosas.

Admire the stunning waterfront views, before concluding your Crab Brunch Experience excursion with another scenic ride back to the pier, soaking in Alaska’s natural beauty one last time before returning to your ship.

commerceProducts.title: Old Town Axe Throwing & Chinatown Pinball

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 0-59% booked

commerceProducts.description: It’s all fun and games (and a twist of local flavor) with this immersive Victoria shore excursion, as you spend the afternoon experiencing a couple of the city’s most exciting highlights, including axe throwing, pinball playing, and Chinatown exploring.
highlights: A narrated drive through Victoria brings you to a private venue in Old Town, where you can enjoy a bit of axe-throwing fun – just remember to aim at the target!

After, embark on a leisurely stroll through Victoria’s vibrant Chinatown, as you enjoy free time to explore; experience the thrill of pinball at Pinhalla Pizzeria, browse the local shops for a unique souvenir, or wander the historic Fan Tan Alley – North America’s narrowest commercial street.
Either way, conclude your Old Town Axe Throwing and Chinatown Pinball excursion with another scenic drive back to port, passing by more downtown highlights along the way.

commerceProducts.title: Craigdarroch Castle & Pendray Scottish Tea

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 0-59% booked

commerceProducts.description: Step back in time with this captivating Victoria shore excursion, as you explore an iconic 19th-century castle, followed by a traditional Scottish tea experience at a beautifully restored mansion.
highlights: Your Victoria immersion begins with a scenic drive along the coastline on your way to Craigdarroch Castle, a Victorian-era Scottish baronial mansion and now a National Historic Site. Here, take the time to embark on a self-guided tour through this impressive mansion, as you admire the centuries of history and grandeur.

After, ride through Victoria’s historic Chinatown before arriving at Pendray Mansion for a traditional Scottish tea experience. Indulge your taste buds with a selection of savory regional treats, including smoked salmon blini, Welsh rarebit, cheddar scones, and more.
Your appetite satisfied, conclude your Craigdarroch Castle and Pendray Scottish Tea excursion with another drive back to port and your awaiting ship.

commerceProducts.title: Evening Tea at the Empress

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 0-59% booked

commerceProducts.description: Indulge in a bit of flavorful luxury and refined elegance with this enchanting shore excursion at the iconic Fairmont Empress Hotel, where you savor a unique tea experience, complemented by a selection of savory bites.
highlights: Following a private drive from port, your experience begins at the historic Fairmont Empress Hotel, a beloved landmark overlooking Victoria’s picturesque Inner Harbour.

Here, settle down within the hotel’s legendary Lobby Lounge and prepare for the ultimate royal treatment, as you sip on your choice of premium hot teas, complete with a signature three-tier stand of Empress scones, cakes, and tea sandwiches.

After savoring this quintessential Victoria tradition, conclude your Evening Tea at the Empress excursion with a narrated tour through the city, soaking in all the amazing highlights as you make your way back to the terminal and your awaiting ship.

commerceProducts.title: Ghosts & Legends of Juneau

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 0-59% booked

commerceProducts.description: Delve into Juneau’s supernatural past with this (allegedly) haunted shore excursion, as you embark on a guided stroll along the wooden boardwalks of Alaska’s capital, where eerie tales of centuries past blend together with scenic coastal charm.
highlights: Your foray into the supernatural begins in historic Juneau, where your guide leads you through its atmospheric downtown district.
As you walk along the charming streets and weathered boardwalks, listen to tales of ghostly apparitions and unexplained encounters, dating back to the city’s Gold Rush era.

At the historic Alaskan Hotel and Bar, the state’s oldest operating hotel, learn of the spectral “fallen woman” said to linger in its halls and the tragic sailor who leapt from a third-floor window - his presence reportedly still felt by guests.
Continue to the legendary Red Dog Saloon, a rowdy relic of frontier days where echoes of miners’ laughter and lingering spirits seem to blend.
Pass by Juneau Drug Company and the historic Silverbow Inn, each with its own whispered stories of unexplained footsteps and shadowy figures.

Along the Totem Pole Trail, your guide decodes Indigenous teachings and symbolism, sharing lore of shape-shifters, sea serpents, Thunderbirds, the elusive Hairy Man, and the mysterious Little People woven into Tlingit tradition.
The tales extend beyond town to the doomed miners of Treadwell Mine’s “Glory Hole” and the lonely beacon of Eldred Rock Lighthouse, where hapless sailors are said to haunt the icy waters.
As you conclude your Ghosts and Legends of Juneau excursion, return to port with a few spooky tales to retell and hopefully no unwanted ghouls following you back home.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Interactive Self-Guided Jeep Tour 4-Door
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a self-guided journey through Juneau's stunning landscapes, all from the comfort and freedom of your own Jeep, as you capture the awe-inspiring beauty of Mendenhall Glacier or savor the tranquility of Eagle Beach.
highlights: Indulge in the freedom of your own four-door Jeep as you venture to Mendenhall Glacier, a magnificent 13-mile expanse of shimmering blue ice nestled against the backdrop of rugged mountains.
Marvel at the glacier's grandeur and witness its dynamic beauty, as it flows into Mendenhall Lake, creating a mesmerizing spectacle of nature's power and grace.
After, embark on a picturesque drive along the Alaska coast to reach the serene Shrine of St. Therese, nestled on a small island surrounded by pristine wilderness.
Discover the tranquility of this sacred site, where a quaint cobblestone church stands amidst towering trees, and keep an eye out for majestic humpback whales feeding in the surrounding waters.

Pause at Eagle Beach, a tranquil oasis where you can unwind at a scenic picnic table and take in the panoramic views of the Chilkat Mountains, while the gentle waters of the Eagle River meander beside you - the perfect backdrop for relaxation and reflection.
Next, traverse the scenic roads of Douglas Island, where breathtaking vistas of Mendenhall Glacier unfold before you, before making your way back to port.

Based on weather and seasonal factors, your guide will choose a fourth location that might include Douglas Island, DiPAC or one of several Juneau highlights. Guests are welcome to choose their own itinerary that the guide will program prior to their departure.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Ocean Raft Wildlife Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Board a US Coast Guard certified open-vessel for an unforgettable high-speed boat adventure in Lynn Canal, North America’s deepest and longest fjord. Outfitted with an exposure suit, you’ll stay warm and dry as you glide across the water and explore hidden coves and waterfalls. Immerse yourself in the wondrous sights of Alaska’s wilderness, while keeping an eye out for local wildlife as you hover close enough to feel the mist of the glacial waterfalls.
highlights: Enjoy a rafting excursion through Lynn Canal, offering an up-close and personal look of Alaska's majestic glacier waterfalls.
Experience possible sightings of Alaskan wildlife such as whales, eagles, seals, mountain goats, and more.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. Guests must consider their physical fitness level and medical history when determining whether this tour is appropriate. Guests who have concerns must consult with their personal physician before engaging in any activity that may be strenuous for them, particularly those involving water-based activities. Guests should consider their swimming ability when selecting tours involving water-based activities.
activityLevel.name: Strenuous

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Zodiac Whale Watching & Wildlife Tour
commerceProducts.description: Hop aboard our high-speed zodiac for a thrilling wildlife-viewing adventure along Victoria's breathtaking west coast. Victoria is considered the world's best location to view whales in the wild. Our Rigid Hull Inflatable Boats bring you closer to the ocean and to the action, immersing you in the magnificent beauty of the Pacific Ocean. Join your skilled captain in search of whales as you zoom through the beautiful waters of the Juan de Fuca and Haro Strait, renowned for their incredible wildlife. Keep on the lookout for transient orcas, humpback whales, Minke whales, seals, sea lions, and marine birds. As you race across the sea, you'll be closer to the elements, giving you a once-in-a-lifetime view of these stunning animals. Our eco-friendly zodiacs are celebrated for their ultra-low emissions, quiet operation, stability, and comfort. Experience an adventure recognized by Surfrider, TripAdvisor, and the Vancouver Island Sustainability Award.
highlights: Marvel at one of the world's most breathtaking coastlines on this thrilling zodiac adventure.
Learn about Canada's stunning west coast wildlife, looking for orcas, humpback whales, seals, sea lions, and birds.
Experience the best location in the world to view whales in the wild.
Race across the sea in search of the wildlife in Juan de Fuca and Haro Strait.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Juneau Salmon Fishing Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Beat the bears to the punch for that dreamed-of trophy salmon, by getting out on the protected inside waterways for a day of sportfishing. Enjoy rod and reel trolling in one of the best fishing grounds in southeast Alaska from the comfort of a charter boat skippered by a U.S. Coast Guard-licensed captain. Additionally, for an extra fee, your fish may be processed and shipped home.  (U.S. addresses only).
highlights: Troll one of the most abundant fishing grounds in Southeast Alaska from the comfort of your charter vessel.
Catch fresh fish in protected inside waters, while marveling at the stunning scenery surrounding you.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Norris Glacier by Helicopter and Dog Sledding
commerceProducts.description: Take to the skies above Juneau aboard a helicopter and fly off to a dog sledding experience on the Norris Glacier. Soar above the Juneau Icefield and enjoy a bird’s-eye view of historic gold mines, alpine lakes, and even your own cruise ship. After a bit of flightseeing, zoom off to the Norris Glacier. Marvel as your pilot touches down on the snowy surface and step out onto an otherworldly landscape. The Norris Glacier is home to a remote camp, only accessible via helicopter, that’s manned by veterans of the legendary Alaskan Iditarod Sled Dog Race. You’ll meet up with these experts for an interactive dog sledding experience. Master the all-important commands of Hike!, Gee! And Haw! Then, feel the thrilling sensation of skidding across the snow covered terrain pulled by a team of Alaskan Sled Dogs. After your adventure, take to the air once again for the panoramic flight home.
highlights: Touch down on the Norris Glacier and meet up with some veterans of the legendary Iditarod.
Fly above the Juneau Icefield for an aerial perspective of this natural wonder.
Experience the thrill of zipping across the glacial surface powered by a team of Alaskan Sled Dogs

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: White Pass Legacy Club Luxury Railway Experience
commerceProducts.description: Experience the dramatic beauty of Southeast Alaska in opulence while traveling aboard the White Pass & Yukon Routes' luxurious Legacy Club Car with your very own private guide. The world-famous narrow gauge White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, revered as the “Scenic Railway of the World”, promises an unforgettable ride along the eastern side of the historic White Pass. And today, you’ll enjoy the exclusive opportunity to ascend over 2,800 feet from sea to Summit in style and comfort while aboard the Legacy Club Car. Retrace the steps of Alaska’s gold seekers as you venture along the centuries-old trail in your luxury car, relishing the custom-made leather seating, all-inclusive beverages, and regionally-sourced appetizers. Throughout your journey, your private guide will highlight all the scenic viewpoints and regale you with the incredible story of the railroad’s beginnings. The White Pass Legacy Club is the ideal way to discover legendary White Pass, with a modern luxury twist.
highlights: Sit back and relax as you travel across Southeast Alaska in your luxury parlor car, enjoying delicious appetizers and all-inclusive beverages along the way.
Join an intimate group of travelers for an exclusive rail experience aboard the legendary White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad’s luxurious Legacy Club Car, complete with your own private guide.

Soak in spectacular views of Alaska’s iconic wilderness as you ascend to the White Pass Summit, passing by breathtaking mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites throughout your journey.
Learn about the impressive history of the railway, built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush and regarded a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.
